amendments.summary: Initial draft: proliferation spending default-FOR under a cap, structural props default-AGAINST, anti-capture, calldata-is-truth integrity, override-driven amendment process.

amendments.trigger: Prop 969 (0 ETH reserve) judged AGAINST — the clause aimed at the reserve-raise freeze also blocked the reserve-removal restoration.
amendments.summary: II.4 'Direction matters': Article II targets freeze-direction changes; structural changes that restore issuance or widen entry default FOR under Article I.

amendments.trigger: Prop 970 (Noun 826 to Gramajo) judged AGAINST — contributor rewards in kind were read as treasury redirection.
amendments.summary: I.5 'Recognition in kind': small treasury-Noun transfers to named contributors with delivered work are mission spending valued at book. I.2 track record now includes unfunded ecosystem work.

amendments.trigger: Prop 962 (501c3 Study) leaned FOR — classify-by-calldata let entity-conversion groundwork read as a small grant.
amendments.summary: II.5 'Reconnaissance is part of the invasion': spending whose deliverable is groundwork for a structural change inherits Article II's default AGAINST, even when the calldata only spends.
